title: Chandrika Devi Temple, Lucknow
tagline: Chandrika Devi Temple, Lucknow — spiritual town of Uttar Pradesh

heroImage.alt: Chandrika Devi Temple, Lucknow, Uttar Pradesh

overview.short: Chandrika Devi Temple is a Hindu temple, dedicated to one of the manifestations of the Hindu goddess Durga, on the bank of river Gomti at Bakshi Ka Talab in Lucknow, Uttar Pradesh.
overview.description: Chandrika Devi Temple is a Hindu temple, dedicated to one of the manifestations of the Hindu goddess Durga, on the bank of river Gomti at Bakshi Ka Talab in Lucknow, Uttar Pradesh. There are nine idols of Durga, who is said to have appeared in the hollow of an old neem tree.

topPlaces.name: Aily
topPlaces.description: Aily is a village in Varanasi district in the Indian state of Uttar Pradesh. It is about 316 kilometres (196 miles) from the state capital Lucknow and 812 kilometres (505 miles) from the national capital Delhi.

topPlaces.name: Subhash Chandra Mahavidyalaya
topPlaces.description: Subhash Chandra Mahavidyalaya is a coeducational postgraduate college in the village of Chakarma, Pindra Tehsil in Varanasi, India. Subhash Chandra Mahavidyalaya started in 2011.

topPlaces.name: Chandrawati Jain temple
topPlaces.description: Chandrawati Jain temple is a Jain tirth located in the Chandrawati village located 23 kilometres (14 mi) from Varanasi. This place is believed to be birthplace of Chandraprabha, the 8th Thirthankara, of Jainism.

topPlaces.name: Swarved Mahamandir
topPlaces.description: Swarved Mahamandir is a charitable meditation centre built in Varanasi and inaugurated by Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi and with its seven-storey claims to be the biggest meditation centre in the World. The centre is built with an estimated cost of Rs 100 crores and has a seating capacity of 20,000 people at a time.

topPlaces.name: Vyaspur
topPlaces.description: Vyaspur is a village in Chiraigaon block of Varanasi district in the Indian state of Uttar Pradesh. It is about 219 kilometers from the state capital Lucknow and 809 kilometers from the national capital Delhi.

items.note: Aily is a village in Varanasi district in the Indian state of Uttar Pradesh. It is about 316 kilometres (196 miles) from the state capital Lucknow and 812 kilometres (505 miles) from the national capital Delhi.
Subhash Chandra Mahavidyalaya is a coeducational postgraduate college in the village of Chakarma, Pindra Tehsil in Varanasi, India. Subhash Chandra Mahavidyalaya started in 2011.

Chandrawati Jain temple is a Jain tirth located in the Chandrawati village located 23 kilometres (14 mi) from Varanasi. This place is believed to be birthplace of Chandraprabha, the 8th Thirthankara, of Jainism.

Swarved Mahamandir is a charitable meditation centre built in Varanasi and inaugurated by Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi and with its seven-storey claims to be the biggest meditation centre in the World. The centre is built with an estimated cost of Rs 100 crores and has a seating capacity of 20,000 people at a time.

Vyaspur is a village in Chiraigaon block of Varanasi district in the Indian state of Uttar Pradesh. It is about 219 kilometers from the state capital Lucknow and 809 kilometers from the national capital Delhi.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Chandrika Devi Temple, Lucknow?
faq.a: The best time to visit Chandrika Devi Temple, Lucknow is Oct – Mar. Summers are 25–42°C and winters are 7–24°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Chandrika Devi Temple, Lucknow?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Lal Bahadur Shastri Airport, Varanasi (18 km) and the nearest railway station is Varanasi Junction (18 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Chandrika Devi Temple, Lucknow?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Chandrika Devi Temple, Lucknow at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Chandrika Devi Temple, Lucknow?
faq.a: Stays in Chandrika Devi Temple, Lucknow start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Chandrika Devi Temple, Lucknow?
faq.a: The top attractions are Aily, Subhash Chandra Mahavidyalaya, Chandrawati Jain temple, Swarved Mahamandir, Vyaspur.
